Okay, welcome back. So this lesson is going to be rather short because it covers a very short part of your sales call, one that's maybe only like 15 or 20 seconds, but for whatever reason has the most anxiety attached to it, which is presenting the investment options, which is really the gateway to actualizing all that potential. So this is often perceived and experienced as the most stressful part of the call for both parties. So I do want to invite you to consider just the possibility that it could be the most exciting, liberating and energizing for you both. Because if everything else has flowed well, being presented with a reasonable, doable, achievable or even easeful way of actualizing all that potential could and should be the most fulfilling and a leading part of the call. So there are really only three steps for presenting the pricing. There could be the first investment option, which is usually a pay in full, then the next would be the payment plan version of it. And then the most important part is your recommendation of what they do next. And that could be including any kind of like code that they may have gotten access to throughout your enrollment process or anything they're eligible for. So this whole process once again takes less than 45 seconds and really doesn't need to be overthought, especially if you've been transparent from the beginning, right, from your application funnel that there is an investment. If you were clear about what that investment range was and someone still shows and gave their consent to being on the call, they gave their consent to hearing about the offer, they got their consent about being presented these options. This should be light, easy, fun, definitely doesn't need to be overthought. So what might this look like when you put it all together for our program, Automated Intimacy, it would look like this. The investment for Automated Intimacy version 2.0 is five grand. But if things feel more useful to split it up into a few payments, we can certainly split that into three payments of 1700. However, really given what you shared earlier about your current revenue levels, and I believe you are awaiting sign off on a few key client projects, I personally recommend going with the plan, which after your past student discount of $1,000 will allow you to get started today. So this is where we would present the pay in full, the payment plan, and what we recommend that integrates what they shared earlier. So it's a very specific, unique recommendation that should match and sync up with some of the things that surfaced throughout our conversation. So the most important part of this presentation of these 30 to 45 seconds is your confidence and clarity in delivering it. So don't project your own financial anxiety or discomfort. Don't create the assumption that this is going to be difficult for them to receive, that it's going to create anxiety in them. When in fact, as we said, if everything is flowing, this could feel exciting and liberating for them, that they have this gateway, this portal to actualize that potential. So get used to stating your price, say it until it just becomes information. It doesn't need to create anxiety. Certainly don't want to bring anxiety to the table that your own prospect isn't feeling themselves. Next most important part is going to be your leadership and recommendation of what's their best move and in basing that on what was shared earlier. So integrating their context with your recommendation for what they should do next, as well as bringing up the why, bringing up any potential concerns you might have about them and tailoring your recommendation based on that. And then simply asking how it lands. This is where you are collaborative, not just dictating what to do, but simply asking like, how does that feel? And you do this versus going silent. Now, a lot of people teach that as soon as you drop the price, you shut up and there's this kind of tense 15 seconds or 20 seconds and 40 seconds or whatever it is of them just mentally processing and you allow them to have that. And that may be okay on some calls, but typically that is going to create more of a confrontational adversarial feeling. It's going to have unnecessary tension, whereas simply asking how that lands, how that feels, feels more collaborative. It really feels like you're on their side of the table trying to work together towards having the most empowered next steps. So what might that look like again? The investment for the 12 week reclaim your energy revamp is three grand. If it feels most useful to split that up, we can certainly do so in three payments of a thousand dollars. As I mentioned earlier in this call, my only concern is that these protocols may be a bit of a shock to your system and that could lead to some initial resistance. That said, I'd feel totally comfortable recommending the payment plan and inviting you in knowing that if things just aren't clicking in those first seven days, we can check back and just hit undo if necessary. With that said, how does getting started at a thousand dollars feel? So once again, bringing up your concerns and then following up with what makes you feel best in them, choosing a certain plan to get started. So once again, in identifying our concern for them, this is what creates safety and trust that we truly do have their back, while also orienting the rest of that conversation or the rest of that conversational thread around a named enemy, a concern that could be mitigated versus the countless unnamed, unmitigated ones that they would create if you didn't bring something else to the surface. So bring up those potential concerns and pitfalls, especially if you do have a way to mitigate them a solution that takes them into account in presenting those investment options. So that is it for presenting the investment options. I told you this would be a short video because it's a short section and hopefully this gives you the confidence to present these fluidly with a leadership based focus, recommending what they should do based on everything you've taken account in the rest of the call. And this is what is going to give you more empowered responses. That said, there will always be certain challenges, concerns and objections that come up both in this phase of the call as well as other phases in this part to actualizing potential part of the call. And in the next video, we are going to walk through what those are so you could recognize them and most importantly, give you some tools, coaching strategies and techniques to overcome them while still being ethical and acting in your prospects best interest. So take a breather and when you're ready, meet me there.
